findings.evidence: Subcoalgebra/Lattice.lean:69-84,97-102,142-184 and Subcomodule/Lattice.lean:79-93,106-111,151-193 prove sSup/CompleteSemilatticeSup from `Submodule.mem_iSup_iff_exists_finsupp`; HopfIdeal.lean:341-403 constructs only binary joins via `Submodule.mem_sup`, and the needed `leftTensorIdeal_mono`/`rightTensorIdeal_mono` (lines 133-141) already suffice for the iSup comul step.
findings.fix: Add `SupSet`/`CompleteSemilatticeSup` instances with carrier `⨆ I : S, I.toIdeal`, using `Submodule.mem_iSup_iff_exists_finsupp` to discharge the closure conditions, and derive the binary `sup`/`SemilatticeSup` from it. Mirror the `sSup_toIdeal`/`mem_sSup`/`iSup_toIdeal`/`mem_iSup` API.
findings.issue: Only binary `Max`/`SemilatticeSup` are provided, but Hopf ideals are closed under arbitrary sums (carrier `⨆ I : S, I.toIdeal`); the comul/counit/antipode conditions all extend over finite sums by `map_sum`, just as the binary instMax extends over `Submodule.mem_sup`. The binary case is then a special case of the general one.

summary: The join API is under-generalized: the same finite-sum argument proves arbitrary suprema of Hopf ideals, and the two sibling lattice files in this codebase already establish CompleteSemilatticeSup as the natural level. Stopping at binary joins forces downstream sSup/iSup/Finset.sup users to reconstruct it.
